_provenance.scope: descriptive — machine-readable mirror of DESIGN.md + extensions (compositional motif catalog, divergence audit, voice, iaPriorities, narrative)
notes: palette is the 10-token Lisbon fairground-pop set authored in BRAND.md § Visual identity
tokens renamed in brand-native vocabulary: chapito-cravo / areia / piri / laranja-doce / azulejo / galinha / malagueta / tejo / noite / cal
palette deployment intensity = `drenched` per intent-dimensions.md § 7.5 (Expressive axis) and per user-documented saturation preference (feedback_palette_saturation_preference.md)
compositional motifs are LOAD-BEARING and not optional — see extensions.motifs.patterns for the named-and-numbered catalog the prototype must reimplement

name: Chapitô
description: Single-maker Portuguese hot-sauce house in Alfama, Lisbon — palette deployed at maximalist intensity, big-top fairground composition vocabulary lifted from the maximalist Italian-pop artisan-craft reference register and rotated to a Lisbon + circus cultural anchor

logotype-medallion.expectedCharacter: classical Didot/Bodoni — high-contrast modern serif, set as circular type around a monogrammatic C with a freestanding circumflex; used only inside the medallion
logotype-medallion.loadBearingRule: wordmark medallion only — never deploy as standing body or display text

display.expectedCharacter: bold condensed all-caps poster sans; fairground-marquee weight, tight tracking, mixed-case FORBIDDEN
rejectedCharacters: italic-classical serif (Playfair italic — that's a different brand)

body.expectedCharacter: humanist neutral grotesque designed for screen reading; mixed-case
rejectedCharacters: serif body (would shift register to editorial)

mono.expectedCharacter: Selectric typewriter character; tight at micro size, loose at marker size; mono load-bearing for ALL numerals

button-circle-cta.purpose: sits at the X-intersection of the marquee ribbon

highlighter-rectangle.purpose: flat solid-color rectangle wrapped around phrase-spans inside body prose; NO shadow, NO gradient, hard edges

marquee-ribbon-tape.purpose: white-tape band spanning edge-to-edge, repeating its phrase in condensed all-caps with · separators

polka-dot-accent.purpose: bottom-left corner accent rectangle in color-block sections

diagonal-stripe-accent.purpose: bottom-right corner accent rectangle in color-block sections

quarantined.rule: highlighter-rectangle accent only + polka-dot accent color + occasional chip-band; never used as a primary section ground
highlighter-rectangle accent + ticket-stub border + 1-2 word body emphasis; never a section ground
sunburst secondary ray color + diagonal-stripe accent ground + occasional ticket-stub fill; never primary text background for body prose

forbidden: Royal-cobalt Lisbon-tile blue (would read as azulejo set-dressing)

typographyMeta.tier: 4-tier: logotype-medallion (Bodoni circular) + display (Anton bold condensed all-caps) + body (Inter humanist sans) + mono (IBM Plex Mono)

tierAssignments.logotype-medallion: Bodoni-style modern serif; load-bearing inside the medallion only; tracking +0.18em; never deployed outside the medallion
tierAssignments.display: Anton bold condensed all-caps; section titles, family banners, CTA labels; mixed-case display FORBIDDEN; italic display FORBIDDEN
tierAssignments.body: Inter (or DM Sans / Outfit) humanist sans; roman primary + italic for highlighter-tape pulls + verbatim Inês-voice quotes
tierAssignments.mono: IBM Plex Mono; micro-labels, ticket-stub tags, dates, addresses, micro-CTAs; ≤ 14px load-bearing

rejectedCharacters: italic-classical serif display (that is a different brand's register)
script web fonts (Pacifico, Permanent Marker, Reenie Beanie — script faces are raster-only on bottle labels)
typographyMeta.loadBearingRule: the brand's surface is fairground-poster condensed-all-caps; any mixed-case display rendering breaks the brand

motion.policy: flat-by-conviction with off-axis rotation + cutout-layering as depth substitute; subtle scroll-reveal + hover micro-interactions per feedback_motion_by_default

paletteDeployment.rationale: brand's central tension is Alentejo slow-cook × Lisbon big-top spectacle; the palette deploys the spectacle. Saturation deployment is load-bearing per BRAND.md § Central Tension. Each product family gets its own vivid ground exactly once; chapito-cravo dominates as the dominant brand surface; chapito-tejo delivers the cover-of-night beat across two consecutive sections (CABEÇUDO family + Trapézio bottle hero).

motifs.rationale: the brand's compositional vocabulary is its load-bearing surface; tokens alone produce slop per feedback_render_divergence_load_bearing.md. Each motif below MUST be reimplemented in any prototype render — they are the brand, not stylistic options.

patterns.purpose: hero section composition — alternating-ray sunburst ground + central circular wordmark medallion + 6 product bottles arranged in a circle + two ticket-stub corner tags
ticketStubs: top-left rotated -6° with PIRI-PIRI label and B&W cutout
top-right rotated +6° with EQUILIBRISTA label and B&W cutout

patterns.purpose: horizontal band with two diagonally-crossed white tape ribbons + central circular CTA button at the X-intersection
centered free-running brand-voice paragraph with 5–7 phrase-spans each wrapped in a hard-edged solid-color highlighter rectangle
rules: NO drop shadow on highlighter rects
NO gradient on highlighter rects
NO rounded corners (≤ 2px)
white text on tejo span

patterns.purpose: full-bleed product-family section composed of: bold condensed all-caps family name top-center · 3-term mono micro-label · 6 bottles in a horizontal row across the visual middle · 3–4 B&W cutout photos rotating ±4° to ±10° · diagonal-stripe accent block bottom-right · polka-dot accent rectangle bottom-left
rules: each family gets its own ground used once
bottles are NEVER rendered as a 3-col card grid — always a single horizontal row
cutout-photo collage is the imagery treatment, never full-bleed photographic background

patterns.purpose: horizontal cluster of small circular cutout-photo chips with colored half-band labels; appears once in the third color-block product section
full-bleed cover-of-night section with single flagship bottle centered + hand-drawn wave-line motif behind + bold condensed name left + mono address block right
centered alone — circular wordmark medallion (same as hero, smaller) + single row of icon-links below (email · IG · WhatsApp · phone)
fixed-bottom 36px band on every page; left: VISITA A LOJA → · right: VAI ATÉ LÁ →

divergence.mode: Mode A — brand-faithful (signal-strong with explicit drenched-deployment + condensed-all-caps display + fairground-collage composition vocabulary declared in BRAND.md)

decade.value: 1950s–60s Portuguese travelling-circus posters × hand-painted Lisbon shopfront signage (cross-decade fusion)

craft.value: B&W cutout-collage photography (Lane 1, load-bearing) + hand-painted bottle labels (Lane 2) + sparing color process photography (Lane 3)

register_flavor.value: Single-maker memoir voice (first-person Inês) crossed with Fairground-Poster (saturated-collage palette deployment + condensed all-caps display + off-axis composition)
register_flavor.picked_by: brand-native: central tension = Alentejo slow-cook × Lisbon big-top spectacle

ground_family.value: solid-color blocks + cutout-collage overlay (NOT atmospheric photographic backgrounds, NOT atmospheric radial gradients as primary depth language)
ground_family.picked_by: user-constraint: BRAND.md § Composition rules + reference visual register

palette.source: inherited from BRAND.md (10-token Lisbon fairground-pop set, brand-native naming)

brand_faithful_inversions.retain: condensed all-caps display over italic-classical serif
brand_faithful_inversions.reason: brand-faithful — fairground-marquee weight is the brand's central display register; italic serif would collapse to a different brand

brand_faithful_inversions.retain: solid-color section grounds over atmospheric photo backgrounds
brand_faithful_inversions.reason: brand-faithful — the brand's signature is cutout-collage ON solid grounds, never photographic surfaces with overlaid type

brand_faithful_inversions.retain: B&W cutout-collage imagery treatment over color landscape
brand_faithful_inversions.reason: brand-faithful — cutout collage IS the brand's surface; lane-1 is load-bearing
user-documented saturation preference + brand-faithful — Drenched deployment is the brand's central thesis

brand_faithful_inversions.retain: off-axis rotation (cutouts, ticket-stubs, marquee, chips) over orthogonal grid
brand_faithful_inversions.reason: brand-faithful — fairground-poster composition is asymmetric and rotated; pure grid would collapse to clean-craft

brand_faithful_inversions.retain: Portuguese-native voice (with English gloss) over English-only
brand_faithful_inversions.reason: brand-faithful — Portuguese is the brand's native language; English-only would collapse to a tourist register

brand_faithful_inversions.retain: all-caps marquee + ticket-stub typography over mixed-case
brand_faithful_inversions.reason: brand-faithful — the fairground vocabulary is unapologetically all-caps poster

brand_faithful_inversions.retain: highlighter-rectangle phrase pulls inside body prose over plain emphasis
brand_faithful_inversions.reason: brand-faithful — the highlighter motif is the brand reading itself out loud; not optional

checked: Hero text on photographic background without contrast scrim
Generic role names in palette (forbidden, brand-native used)
3-col card grid for products (forbidden, single horizontal bottle row used)
Italian liquor-maker pastiche (Opificio / Liquoreria / Cattaneo) — refused at every layer
Tourist-Lisbon clichés (fado / saudade / azulejo as set-dressing / pastel de nata / bacalhau as atmosphere) — refused
Slow Food / Cereal-Magazine terracotta-luxe — refused via saturated palette + cutout-collage register
Wes Anderson symmetric pastel — refused via off-axis rotation
Brooklyn industrial-craft — refused via Portuguese fairground vocabulary
Script web-fonts as standing text — refused (script lives only on raster bottle labels)
Italic-classical serif as display — refused (Anton condensed load-bearing)
antiToolboxAudit.result: clean — all checked, none required removal

thin-fixed-footer.purpose: persistent 36px bar at viewport bottom; mono all-caps left + right CTA pair

sunburst-medallion.purpose: hero composition unit — alternating-ray ground + central circular wordmark + radial bottle arrangement + ticket-stub corner tags

marquee-x-band.purpose: horizontal band with X-crossed tape ribbons + center circular CTA

highlighter-manifesto.purpose: centered manifesto paragraph with highlighter-rectangle phrase pulls

color-block-section.purpose: full-bleed product-family band; family name + bottle row + cutout collage + corner accents

chip-tag-cluster.purpose: loose horizontal cluster of circular cutout-photo chips with colored half-band labels

big-dark-bottle.purpose: cover-of-night flagship bottle section; dark ground + wave-line motif + single bottle

voice.register: slow plainspoken declaratives; specifics over modifiers; reverent about the food, playful about the spectacle; bilingual where bilingual makes sense (Portuguese-native, English gloss)
do: Specifics over modifiers — 'Costa do Castelo, número 7', 'seis semanas de fermentação', 'frasco de 100 ml', 'ano após ano'.
Reverent about the food. Playful about the spectacle. Both at once.
Portuguese is the brand's native language; English is the gloss, not the default.
dont: we / our team / passionate / crafted / curated / artisanal / handcrafted / award-winning / finest
adjective stacks of three or more
Tourist-Lisbon clichés as decoration — fado / saudade / azulejo (as tile-set-dressing) / pastel de nata / bacalhau (as atmosphere)
Costume-Portuguese — obrigado plate, anchor-rope nautical pastiche
Italian liquor-maker pastiche of any kind
naming or referencing the reference brand (Opificio Cattaneo) — anywhere

do: As etiquetas são barulhentas porque a cozinha é silenciosa.
dont: Our passionate team of artisans crafts each bottle with care.
A taste of saudade in every bottle.
Crafted with love in the Lisbon sunshine.
Atelier Inês — a journal of Lisbon spice.

rules: Voice is quiet against the loud palette. Saturation × restraint is the brand's tension.
Portuguese-native register; English serves as gloss only.
Reverent about the food, playful about the spectacle. Never wink at the food.
First-person singular everywhere except legal/utility footer.
No Italian language. No reference to the visual-register source brand by name.

iaPriorities.evidence: home page leads with the sunburst medallion + the persistent fixed bottom bar carrying VAI ATÉ LÁ → linking to .shop subdomain. The X-marquee section delivers a center-button VAI À LOJA as the page's most prominent CTA.
iaPriorities.preserveAs: first-fold visual anchor + fixed bottom CTA on every page

narrative.northStar: Big-top spectacle wrapping a quiet pepper farm. A 1962 Portuguese travelling-circus poster wheat-pasted over the door of an Alentejo curing shed.
narrative.overview: Chapitô is a single-maker Portuguese hot-sauce house in Alfama, Lisbon. The visual system deploys a saturated 10-token Lisbon fairground-pop palette across 6 distinct section grounds on the home page, condensed all-caps display throughout, cutout B&W collage imagery over solid color blocks, and a named-and-numbered compositional motif catalog (sunburst medallion · X-crossed marquee · highlighter-tape manifesto · color-block product collages · chip-tag cluster · big-dark bottle hero · medallion footer · persistent bottom bar). The central tension is Alentejo slow-cook × Lisbon big-top spectacle — quiet kitchen, loud labels.
keyCharacteristics: 10 brand tokens deployed across 6 distinct section grounds on the home page (Drenched palette deployment)
Asymmetric cycling between section grounds — no strict A/B alternation
Condensed all-caps display (Anton) at all section titles, family banners, CTA labels — mixed-case display FORBIDDEN
Italic body emphasis reserved for highlighter-tape pulls and Inês-voice quotes
IBM Plex Mono for ticket-stub tags, micro-labels, dates, addresses, addresses — ≤ 14px load-bearing
Solid-color section grounds + B&W cutout-collage overlay + off-axis rotation as the depth language (NOT atmospheric photo backgrounds, NOT radial gradients as primary depth)
Eight named compositional motifs (see extensions.motifs.patterns) reimplemented in every render — load-bearing, not optional
rules: The Saturation-Deployment Rule — 6 distinct section grounds; asymmetric cycling required; each product family gets its own ground exactly once.
The Cutout-Collage Rule — B&W cutout photography on solid grounds; never full-bleed photo background with overlaid type.
The Condensed-All-Caps Display Rule — Anton bold condensed all-caps for display; mixed-case display forbidden; italic display forbidden.

The Off-Axis Rotation Rule — cutouts, ticket-stubs, marquee ribbons, accent rectangles, chip-tag clusters all sit at angles -12° to +12°; pure orthogonal layout forbidden except in centered display moments.
The Highlighter-Tape Rule — flat solid-color rectangles wrap brand-voice phrase-spans inside body prose; no shadow, no gradient, no rounded corners.
The Mono-As-Marker Rule — IBM Plex Mono ≤ 14px only; numerals always render in mono.
The Native-Portuguese Rule — Portuguese is the brand's native language; English is gloss, never default.
The No-Italian Rule — no Italian language, no Italian-place reference, no Italian liquor pastiche; the reference brand is the visual register, not a source of words or places.
The No-Pure-Black-or-White Rule — chapito-noite #1a1410 darkest; chapito-cal #fffaef lightest.
IA-priority — single primary CTA destination (the .shop subdomain) reinforced across hero medallion + X-marquee center button + persistent bottom bar.
dos: Deploy palette across 6 distinct section grounds, each product family on its own ground.
Use condensed all-caps display (Anton) for every display tier.
Use cutout B&W collage over solid grounds — never photo background.
Render every named motif from extensions.motifs.patterns.
Rotate cutouts, ticket-stubs, ribbons, and accent rectangles off-axis.
Wrap brand-voice phrase-spans in highlighter rectangles.
Keep Portuguese as the brand's native language.
donts: Render italic-classical serif as display (that is High Lonesome's register, not Chapitô's).
Name or reference the visual-register source brand.
Render a 3-col card grid for the bottles.
Use the Slow-Food / Cereal-Magazine terracotta-luxe palette.
Render atmospheric photographic backgrounds with overlaid type (use solid grounds + cutout collage instead).
Use script web-fonts as standing text (script is raster-only on bottle labels).
